author: claude
content: HATER REVIEW (read-only, draft #1209) — VERDICT: Acceptable. Crash fix is SOUND: regular file removes the EPIPE mechanism (not just the window); verified at OS level; both teardown paths (graceful os._exit + forced SIGKILL) covered; no surviving FIFO/stderr EPIPE path. BUT two real defects gate merge: (1 MAJOR) temp-file LEAK on every teardown — unlink now lives only in run()'s finally, which never runs under os._exit() (daemon thread, no join) or SIGKILL. Original unlinked on first log line, eagerly+unconditionally. Long crawls leak thousands of owpm_driver_*.log in /tmp. Fix: unlink-on-open (after writer opens line 155); anonymous inode carried by open fds, leak-proof on all paths, deletes the drain dance. (2 MAJOR) stop()+drain is unjoined dead code — nothing waits for the drain before process exit. Plus (MINOR) regular-file readline() fragments lines split across flushes/polls into 2 log lines (debug-fidelity regression); (MINOR) zero test coverage for FirefoxLogInterceptor. Recommend: adopt unlink-on-open, delete stop()/drain, add 1 pyonly test. Full writeup: /home/vringar/projects/OpenWPM/datadir/hater-1209.md
